stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1872, Hudson River School painter Albert Bierstadt completed Mount Corcoran.
  • His expansive painting (61 × 96 inches) depicts a snow-capped Sierra Nevada peak above a calm alpine lake.
  • In 2017, Diné artist Emmi Whitehorse completed Lake Powell Suite.
  • Whitehorse’s expansive painting (60 × 96 inches) was inspired in part by Bierstadt’s tradition of grand landscape painting.
  • It evokes the desert Southwest through layered, semi-abstract forms.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two paintings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Bierstadt, a Hudson River School painter, finished his painting in 1872; Whitehorse, a Diné artist, completed hers in 2017.
Although Whitehorse’s painting was inspired by Bierstadt’s tradition, the landscapes the two paintings portray are very different.
Bierstadt’s painting depicts a Sierra Nevada peak above an alpine lake, while Whitehorse’s evokes the desert Southwest through layered, semi-abstract forms.
Bierstadt’s and Whitehorse’s paintings are both expansive, measuring 61 × 96 inches and 60 × 96 inches, respectively.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the two paintings, noting that Bierstadt’s painting (61 × 96 inches) and Whitehorse’s painting (60 × 96 inches) are both expansive in size.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence notes when each painting was completed; it doesn’t emphasize a similarity between them. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes differences rather than a similarity. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between the two paintings rather than a similarity.
